title: Crown the Pawn
setup: The white pawn has reached **a7** — one step from a brand-new queen.
hint: Always promote with check when you can. It denies the defender a free move.
explanation:
## The Idea

A simple **promotion with check**. The pawn on a7 reaches a8 and transforms into a queen, simultaneously delivering check along the eighth rank to the king on e8.

White plays {{a8=Q+}}.

## Black's Forced Reply

The black king must address the check. With no defenders nearby, the only options are king moves:

- {{Kd7}}, {{Ke7}}, {{Kf7}}, {{Kf8}}, {{Kd8}} — all flight squares are valid.
- The king cannot reach a8 to capture (too far).
- Nothing can interpose — Black has no other pieces.

White emerges with a queen plus the king — a trivially won endgame.

## Pattern to Memorize

> When promoting, **prefer queening with check** — it forces the opponent to react to your move first.

Watch out for stalemate traps in slightly different positions: when the enemy king has no legal squares, a non-checking queen can accidentally draw. Here, the king has plenty of breathing room, so the check is pure profit.
